title: About
text:
Research Interests
Broadly speaking, I am interested in the applications of mathematical analysis, topology, and geometry in quantitative finance. Some specific topics of interest are:

\(H^\infty\) control and optimization
Information geometry
Numerical methods for obstacle problems

My interest in \(H^\infty\) control theory stems from my research work with Dr. Valentin Matache during my undergrad at UNO, where I studied composition operators on Hardy spaces and Pick interpolation.
More recently, my colleague, Nicholas Christophides, and I, attempted to incorportate an FDM approach solving a free-boundary PDE modeling option pricing with proportional transacation costs for a class project in order to validate the results produced by solving the FBPDE using physics-informed neural networks (PINN). The free-boundary PDE of our interest was equivalent to a double-obstacle problem.
Otherwise, my interest in information geometry is due to a long-time enjoyment of studying topology and geometry.
I anticipate my work will focus on one of the three subjects above as I advance through the PhD program.

title: Jacob White
text:
I’m a first year PhD student interested in the applications of complex analysis and differential geometry to quantitative finance.
I write about topics I’m interested in here.
